brief: Instagram Growth Manager: Launch & Scale Healthy Snack Brand (France)  Project Overview  We are launching a brand-new Instagram account for AERI Makhana (a premium, healthy popped water lily seed snack) targeting the French market. We need an aggressive, creative growth expert to take this account from 0 to a target milestone of 100k genuine French followers. The strategy must balance hyper-growth with community building. We want real engagement, not empty bot numbers. Because we are a new brand, we are operating on a tight startup budget. We need someone who can maximize organic reach, viral loops, and low-cost/barter collaborations.  Scope of Work (What You Will Do) 1. Profile Setup & Optimization: Complete optimization for the French market (bio, highlights, localized SEO, geo-tagging). 2. Content Creation & Management: Develop a 30-day content calendar. Edit/repurpose our raw assets into highly shareable, viral-potential Reels using trending French audio, aesthetic carousel posts, and interactive Stories. 3. Low-Cost Influencer Outreach: Identify, pitch, and secure barter deals (product in exchange for content) with micro-creators in the French food, fitness, and healthy lifestyle niches. 4. Organic Growth Strategy: Implement high-yield growth tactics (giveaways, community management, viral hooks, and heavy engagement with French healthy-snacking communities). 5. Analytics: Provide weekly reports tracking follower growth, reach, engagement rate, and bio-link clicks.  We will supply: Raw product images, brand colors, guidelines, and raw video footage.  Acceptance Criteria 1. Target Audenced Location: 90%+ of gained followers must be located in France. 2. High Engagement: Maintain a strong engagement rate (saves, shares, comments) to ensure the algorithm favors our content. 3. Brand Integrity: All content must be high-quality, professional, in fluent French, and perfectly aligned with our healthy-snacking identity.  How to Apply (Proposal Requirements) To be considered, please keep your proposal brief and directly answer the following questions: 1. Going from 0 to 100k is a massive mountain to climb on a budget. What is your specific, realistic strategy to achieve explosive, organic growth for a food brand in France within the first 30–60 days? 2. What style of Reels or content hooks do you find perform best right now in the French healthy-food niche? 3. Do you have an existing network or a proven script for getting French micro-influencers to agree to barter deals? 4. What is your all-inclusive flat monthly rate for this project? (Keep our startup budget constraints in mind).

You’ll receive loose briefs or rough footage from me; your task is to polish them into ready-to-publish posts, suggest titles and hashtags, and deliver final files in platform-friendly resolutions. Deliverables for each post • Edited vertical video (MP4, 1080×1920, under 60 s)   • Captions burned in and a caption file (.srt)   • Suggested title, description, and three to five hashtags    I’m aiming for an upbeat, conversational vibe that feels authentic rather than corporate, so creativity and an eye for trends are essential. brief: We are a Canadian brand and creative agency looking for a reliable, experienced production designer to join us as a long-term subcontract partner on a retail client engagement. This is not conceptual work. This is structured, brief-driven production at high volume. If you are fast, consistent, brand-disciplined, and comfortable working within tight guidelines on a repeatable basis, this role is built for you. --- **What the work looks like** You will receive creative briefs from our team with clear direction, approved brand assets, and defined specifications. Your job is to execute accurately and efficiently across a range of digital and print formats. Asset types include: - Email headers and banners - Paid social and display ads - Organic social posts and stories - E-commerce homepage tiles, hero banners, and product pages - In-store signage and retail graphics - New product launch assets across multiple tiers Volume runs up to 600 assets per month across approximately 40 briefs. Work is predominantly static design with resizing of master designs into channel-specific dimensions. --- **What we need from you** - Strict adherence to existing brand guidelines. Consistency across every asset is non-negotiable. - Accurate resizing and exporting across all required formats and dimensions. - Delivery of production-ready files and editable working files in required formats and naming conventions. - Standard turnaround of 2 to 4 weeks on most briefs. - Availability for occasional 24 to 48 hour rush turnarounds when required. - Clear, responsive communication with our internal team. --- **You are a fit if you have:** - A strong portfolio showing retail, e-commerce, or consumer brand production work - Demonstrated experience with high-volume, multi-format asset production - Expert proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite, specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign - Experience working within strict brand guidelines on behalf of a client or agency - Top Rated or Top Rated Plus status on Upwork - North American time zone or significant overlap --- **What this is not** This is not a branding or concept role. We handle creative direction and strategy. We need a production partner who executes that direction with precision and speed. If you are looking for conceptual freedom, this is not the right fit. --- **Engagement details** This is a long-term subcontract position tied to an ongoing client engagement. We are looking for someone who wants consistent, predictable volume over time, not one-off projects. We will start with a paid test brief of approximately 10 assets to assess fit before committing to the full engagement. --- **To apply** Please include: - Portfolio samples showing retail, e-commerce, or high-volume production work - A brief note on your experience with brand-guideline-driven production - Your hourly rate - Your typical availability and time zone

brief: Role Overview The client is seeking a highly organized and proactive Digital Marketing & Executive Support Coordinator to provide direct support to the Managing Director across website management, product content updates, SharePoint administration, marketing collateral, social media, digital organization, and general business administration. This role serves as a key behind-the-scenes support function, ensuring that business operations, product information, marketing materials, and digital platforms remain accurate, current, and professionally maintained. The successful candidate will work closely with the Managing Director and internal travel team. The ideal person will be technically capable, detail-oriented, self-motivated, and comfortable managing a diverse range of digital, marketing, and administrative responsibilities. This multifaceted role combines WordPress website management, Flatsome/UX Builder content updates, SharePoint administration, supplier and product updates, marketing collateral creation, social media support, digital organization, and general business administration. Key Responsibilities 1. Executive Support Provide day-to-day administrative and operational support to the Managing Director. Assist with business projects, marketing initiatives, supplier communications, and internal administration. Prepare, format, and update documents, spreadsheets, reports, and business resources. Maintain action lists, monitor outstanding tasks, and help ensure projects progress to completion. Assist with email drafting, document formatting, file management, and general coordination 2. Website & WordPress Management Update and maintain company websites using WordPress. Manage website content using Flatsome and UX Builder. Create, edit, and maintain resort pages, holiday package pages, wedding pages, landing pages, and general website content. Upload and format images, PDFs, flyers, and promotional materials. Perform basic website modifications using HTML and CSS. Review website functionality, including links, formatting, page layouts, and mobile responsiveness. Implement basic SEO updates, including: Page titles Meta descriptions Image alt text Internal linking Identify outdated content, broken links, formatting issues, and missing information, escalating concerns to the Managing Director. Ensure all website content remains accurate, current, and aligned with brand standards. 3. SharePoint Intranet & Product Information Management Update and maintain product information within Microsoft SharePoint. Upload, organize, and manage supplier updates, resort information, rate sheets, product flyers, and training materials. Ensure internal product information remains current and accessible for the travel consultant team. Create and update product summaries, supplier notes, and reference documentation. Maintain consistency between website content, SharePoint resources, and marketing collateral. Ensure product information is clearly organized and easily accessible. Support version control and digital file management across SharePoint and OneDrive. 4. Supplier & Product Administration Review supplier and resort updates and convert them into clear website, intranet, and marketing collateral updates. Maintain accurate product information across both internal and external platforms. Update offers, resort inclusions, package details, supplier notes, and product documentation. Support the Managing Director with supplier-related administration and communications. Help ensure the travel team has access to accurate and up-to-date product information. 5. Marketing Collateral Development Create, update, and maintain marketing materials, including: Wedding flyers Resort flyers Holiday package flyers Agent flyers Client-facing documents Product update sheets Promotional PDFs Internal reference materials Additional responsibilities include: Updating existing templates and creating new collateral as required. Preparing materials for digital distribution, email campaigns, website uploads, and print production. Ensuring all collateral is accurate, visually professional, and aligned with company branding. Assisting with image resizing, PDF preparation, and document presentation. 6. Social Media Support Assist with creating, scheduling, and publishing content across social media platforms. title: Restaurant Menu Redesign
note: Connector can export/proof and swap copy on the client AI file, but the real ask is editorial reflow of a dense tri-fold (reorganise ramen, add Wagyu section without crowding) and packaging editable AI source with fonts — a human designer must do the layout reflow and pre-press.

brief: Project Overview We are a ramen and sushi restaurant called House of Umami. We are updating our existing printed menu and need a professional designer to revise the layout while incorporating new menu items and pricing updates. The goal is not a full redesign, but rather an updated, clean version of our existing menu that fits additional items without feeling crowded, especially on the ramen section. Our current menu is a tri-fold printed menu (22.5” x 12” unfolded) used for dine-in customers. Scope of Work The designer will: Update our existing menu design Insert new menu items Remove discontinued items Update prices Adjust categories where needed Optimize the layout Ensure all content fits cleanly within the tri-fold format Improve readability and flow Avoid overcrowding (especially in the ramen section) Reorganize sections if necessary Current menu sections include: Small Plates Sushi Bowls Artisan Ramen Donburi Rice Bowls Sushi Rolls Nigiri / Sashimi Kids Desserts Solve the biggest layout challenge We are adding several ramen items and a Wagyu section. The designer should thoughtfully reorganize the ramen area so it remains clear and easy to read. Maintain our current brand style The current menu has: Dark background Food photography Red accent elements Japanese ramen/sushi aesthetic We want the same general vibe, but cleaner and more professional. Deliverables Final deliverables should include: • Print-ready PDF (CMYK, 300 DPI) • Editable source file (Adobe Illustrator or InDesign preferred) • Fonts and linked assets included • Correct bleed and margins for print Menu size: 22.5” x 12” unfolded tri-fold (this can be adjusted if needed to fit content) Files Provided We will provide: • Current menu PDF + AI file for current menu • Document outlining menu changes • Updated item descriptions and prices • Existing brand visuals What We’re Looking For Ideal candidates should have: • Experience designing restaurant menus • Strong typography and layout skills • Ability to organize large amounts of menu content clearly • Experience designing tri-fold or multi-panel menus Success Criteria The final menu should: • Fit all new content cleanly • Be easy for guests to scan and order from • Highlight ramen and signature items effectively • Maintain a premium, modern ramen-house aesthetic Timeline Looking to complete within 2-4 days after hiring. To Apply Please include: • 2–3 examples of restaurant menu designs you’ve created • Confirmation you can deliver editable files • Estimated turnaround time

brief: Graphic Designer — Contract Remote-friendly · Ongoing · Figma required · Start ASAP Everlab is Australia's leading preventive health and longevity clinic. We're a fast-moving marketing team with a strong visual identity, a growing content pipeline, and a lot on our plate. We're looking for a sharp contract designer to plug in immediately and help us execute across social, email, and ad formats — no hand-holding required. What you'll be working on Social content — Feed posts, stories, and carousel assets across Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn. Channel-appropriate sizing and formatting is a must — you should know what performs where. EDMs — Email design assets and templates aligned to our brand. Clean, elevated layouts that hold up at small sizes and in dark mode. Paid ad creative — Static ad assets for Meta campaigns across multiple formats and sizes. Experience with performance-driven design is a bonus. Ad hoc requests — Presentations, one-pagers, partner assets, and anything else the team needs turned around quickly. Volume is real — flexibility is key. What we need from you Figma proficiency — You live in Figma. Components, auto-layout, shared styles — second nature. Brand discipline — We have a defined style guide. You'll work within it — not around it. Elevated design eye — You know the difference between good and great. Health and wellness aesthetics are a plus. Channel awareness — You understand how design behaves differently across Instagram, email, and paid — and you design accordingly. Responsive and reliable — Fast turnarounds, clear communication, no chasing required. High volume comfort — We have a lot on. You can manage a queue, prioritise, and produce at pace. About the engagement Hours are flexible — we're not committing to a fixed weekly number upfront because our output needs fluctuate. What we can say is there's consistent work, and the right person will find there's plenty to get into. We're open to discussing a day-rate or hourly structure depending on your preference. This is a BAU-first role, but it touches the full breadth of our marketing output. If you're motivated by quality output, enjoy working in a tight-knit team, and like seeing your work go live quickly — this is a good fit.

brief: We are looking for a talented graphic designer to create 4 high-converting, scroll-stopping static image ads for a Food & Beverage brand focused on strong performance marketing across Meta (Facebook & Instagram). These ads will be used for paid social media advertising campaigns, so experience in direct-response creative design and performance marketing is highly preferred. Our goal is to create visuals that don’t just look good — they capture attention immediately, communicate value fast, and drive conversions. We are looking for a designer who understands how to create ads that stop the scroll within 1–2 seconds and are optimized for CTR and engagement. Project Scope Create 4 static image ads, each targeting a different food & beverage angle: 1. Product Highlight / Hero Offer Ad Target Goal: Showcase the main product in an appetizing, high-impact way that instantly grabs attention. Focus on: Visual appeal (texture, freshness, quality) Strong headline or hook Clear product positioning 2. Lifestyle / Emotional Consumption Ad Target Goal: Connect the product with a lifestyle moment (comfort, enjoyment, sharing, energy, etc.). Focus on: Emotional storytelling Relatable real-life scenarios Strong visual mood (warm, premium, or refreshing depending on brand) 3. Problem → Solution Ad Target Goal: Highlight a common food-related problem and position the product as the solution. Examples: “Hungry but want something healthy?” “Need a quick, satisfying snack?” “Tired of unhealthy fast options?” Focus on: Clear pain point Simple value proposition Strong before/after messaging style 4. Offer / Conversion Ad Target Goal: Drive direct action (purchase, order, or visit). Focus on: Limited-time offer or promotion Strong CTA (Shop Now / Order Today) Clean, conversion-focused layout Deliverables 4 static Meta ad creatives (Facebook & Instagram optimized) Proper sizing for all placements Editable source files (Canva preferred or Adobe files accepted) Up to 2 revision rounds included Style Direction We are looking for designs that are: Bold and visually striking Clean and modern Highly appetizing / visually engaging Optimized for scroll-stopping performance Built with strong visual hierarchy Avoid generic templates or low-effort stock-style designs. Strong emphasis on: Attention-grabbing hooks Clear messaging hierarchy Emotional or sensory triggers (taste, freshness, craving) Conversion-focused layout and CTA placement Assets Provided We will provide: Brand guidelines (logo, colors, fonts if available) Product photos/videos Copy and messaging direction Offer details (if applicable) Designers may use additional copyright-safe assets where needed to enhance visual quality. Preferred Experience Meta / Facebook / Instagram ad creative design Food & beverage marketing or e-commerce ads Direct-response advertising understanding Strong grasp of CTR / conversion-focused creative principles Application Requirement Please include examples of previous Meta ad creatives or performance marketing work, especially in food, beverage, or consumer product niches. We are looking for a designer who understands that great ads are not just designed — they are engineered to make people stop scrolling and take action.

input_assets: Large close-up portrait of Mariya (calm-but-powerful expression) | Very short emotionally-charged text (2-6 words, e.g. 'The Portal Has Opened') | Palette: gold, cream, deep plum, black, rose, cosmic blue, candlelight; dark moody backgrounds | Premium typography + subtle sacred-geometry/cosmic textures | Deliverable: reusable thumbnail system (typography + colour + facial-treatment style), 3-5 variants/video for CTR testing
brief: Channel Reference: https://www.youtube.com/@ThisisMariya/videos GOAL: Create highly clickable YouTube thumbnails with strong CTR potential while maintaining a premium, spiritual, feminine, intelligent, and trustworthy aesthetic. The thumbnails should feel mystical and emotionally activating — NOT cheesy, gimmicky, fake “guru,” or over-saturated spiritual cliché. The channel is about: spirituality consciousness energy manifestation divine feminine healing portals / energetic updates esoteric wisdom transformation CORE CREATIVE DIRECTION: The thumbnails should feel: magnetic cinematic sacred emotionally charged elegant mysterious modern spiritual high-frequency premium YouTube-native Avoid: cheap “law of attraction” aesthetics excessive glowing eyes / fake lightning rainbow overload clutter generic Canva spiritual style stock-photo coaching vibe forced clickbait expressions The best reference energy is: “luxury spiritual cinema” + “mystical documentary” + “divine feminine oracle” THUMBNAIL PRIORITIES (MOST IMPORTANT): Immediate emotional intrigue Curiosity gap Human connection through Mariya’s face/eyes Strong readability on mobile Clear visual hierarchy Consistent recognizable brand identity Feels elevated and intentional VISUAL STYLE: Dark/moody backgrounds with luminous highlights Gold, cream, deep plum, black, rose, cosmic blue, candlelight tones Ethereal lighting Soft glow accents used minimally and tastefully Cinematic contrast Depth and atmosphere Sacred geometry / cosmic textures used subtly Premium typography only FACE & EXPRESSION DIRECTION: Mariya should look: calm but powerful deeply knowing emotionally present magnetic spiritually activated compassionate yet intense Avoid exaggerated YouTube shock faces. TEXT STYLE: Very short text (2–6 words ideal) Large and instantly readable on mobile Use emotionally charged phrases Text should create curiosity, urgency, or revelation Examples: “The Portal Has Opened” “You’re Awakening” “This Changes Everything” “The Energy Shift Is Here” “Why You Feel Different” “The Divine Feminine Returns” COMPOSITION: Preferred: Large close-up portrait Strong focal point Minimal distractions One dominant emotional idea Cinematic cropping Clean separation between subject/background CTR PSYCHOLOGY: Thumbnail should trigger: curiosity emotional resonance spiritual significance transformation exclusivity mystery “I NEED to know this” It should feel like: “This video contains hidden wisdom specifically for me.” BRAND CONSISTENCY: All thumbnails should look like part of one recognizable universe. A viewer should instantly recognize a Mariya thumbnail without reading the channel name. IMPORTANT: Do NOT optimize for generic YouTube trends. Optimize for: spiritual sophistication emotional depth trust mystery feminine power premium visual identity DELIVERABLE IDEALLY: Reusable thumbnail system/template Typography system Color system Facial treatment style 3–5 thumbnail variations per video for CTR testing

task_type: Vinyl Branding Sticker Set — multiple sizes (shelf to window)
title: Vinyl Shop Branding Sticker Design
note: Express templates + logo placement + vectorize/resize produce the decals; proper print prep (cut lines, bleed, lamination spec) and a bespoke creative concept beyond template assets are finished by a human/print shop.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express sticker/decal template reflecting logo palette) -> asset_add_file (store logo) -> fill_text (any store copy) -> image_crop_and_resize (scale to each size: small shelf label up to large window piece) -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (AI/SVG) -> document_convert_pdf (press-ready per size)
input_assets: Current store logo files + brand colour palette (exact hex to confirm) | List of required sizes (small shelf labels through large window/facade pieces) | Any text/copy to appear on the decals | Outputs: final vector AI/SVG + press-ready PDF per approved size with bleed/trim/cut lines; placement mock-up usage sheet | Production note: high-tack weather-resistant vinyl, UV/scratch lamination recommendations (advisory — confirm with print shop)
brief: I’m refreshing my store’s look and need a set of eye-catching vinyl stickers that will carry our branding consistently across glass doors, counters, product displays, and even the exterior façade. Because these decals must perform both indoors and out, durability and colour fidelity matter just as much as great design.    Here’s what I’m after: a creative concept that reflects our current logo palette, scalable layouts for several sizes (small shelf labels up to large window pieces), and print-ready artwork optimised for high-tack, weather-resistant vinyl. If you routinely prep files for digital printing—complete with bleed, trim marks and cut lines—you’ll feel right at home on this project.  Deliverables should include: • Two initial design directions with room for feedback   • Final vector artwork (AI/SVG) and a press-ready PDF for each approved size   • A simple usage sheet showing placement mock-ups so my installer knows where everything goes    Experience with vinyl sticker production is a plus; knowledge of proper lamination recommendations for UV and scratch protection will help my print shop turn these around quickly. Let me know your turnaround time and how many revision rounds you’re comfortable including. Looking forward to seeing how you can turn this branding into bold, enduring stickers!

brief: I need our existing beverage sticker taken from “good enough” to truly shelf-worthy. The café has three bottled items—Cold Coffee, Cold Cocoa and an Ice Tea—and each share the same core layout today. I want a full refresh that keeps the size and basic die-cut shape but upgrades everything else: colour palette, font choice and visual accents.  Font: the current typeface feels dated and heavy. I’d like something cleaner, balanced and legible at arm’s length when the bottles sit in a cooler.  Colour scheme: each flavour should feel distinct yet obviously from the same brand family. You’re free to propose new tones that complement a warm café identity while still popping against condensation and low-light fridges.  Imagery: I’m leaning toward abstract graphics rather than literal product photos or stereotypical café icons. Think subtle patterns, shapes or textures that evoke cool refreshment and give each sticker memorable shelf presence.  Deliverables • Three print-ready sticker files (AI or layered PSD plus press-quality PDF)   • One mock-up of each design on a bottle for quick stakeholder approval   • Colour, font and graphic usage guide so we can replicate the look across future SKUs  I’ll provide the current artwork, logo files and exact die-line the moment we start. The sticker must feature two things only: our company logo in a visually balanced position and the essential battery specifications (voltage, capacity, chemistry, model number). No extra graphics or copy—just crisp typography, generous white space, and a layout that feels contemporary rather than decorative.    Size will follow the standard battery housing label area (I’ll share exact millimetre dimensions once we start). Please keep the colour palette limited to our corporate black, white, and one accent tone pulled from the brand guide; gradients or busy backgrounds are out.    Deliverables   • Print-ready PDF with 3 mm bleed and crop marks   • Editable source file (AI or layered PSD) with outlined fonts and linked assets   • A quick mock-up on a cylindrical cell image so I can visualise placement    If you think a tiny compliance icon would balance the design without cluttering it, let me know, but it isn’t mandatory. File hand-off will be complete once the artwork passes my in-house test print for legibility at 100 % scale.

brief: Here's a fuller brief for the designer: Book Cover Design Brief - Speedrunning Capitalism Hello, I have finished writing my book and require a front cover designed, with a back cover to follow once the physical edition is confirmed. The Book Title: Speedrunning Capitalism Subtitle: How Business Acquisition Accelerates Your Wealth Author: Brandon Lipman Speedrunning Capitalism is a business acquisition guide for the middle class. It's direct, practical and unapologetic. Not a traditional business book - no corporate tone, no fluff. Think closer to Alex Hormozi's visual aesthetic than a Harvard Business Review publication. Current Draft I have attached a draft cover I've put together in Canva. You're welcome to improve on this layout or take it in a completely different creative direction - I'm open to both. The Image The current draft uses an arrow breaking through a horizontal line. The concept is: something fast, precise and powerful breaking through a ceiling or orbit. The arrow works as a metaphor for both a traditional arrow and a rocket - deliberately ambiguous. I specifically don't want a rocket. Rockets are overused on business books and too literal. The arrow-breaking-through-a-line concept captures the same idea without being cliche. If you have a stronger creative idea that captures the same energy - acceleration, breaking through, upward trajectory - I'm open to seeing it. Design Direction Dark background - current draft uses near-black (#1a1a1a) White title text, gold accent (#C8A84B) for author name bar and details Clean, bold, typographic - the title should dominate SPEEDRUNNING on one line, CAPITALISM on the next line below it - these are the two biggest elements on the cover Author name: Brandon Lipman Subtitle at the bottom: How Business Acquisition Accelerates Your Wealth Should feel confident and modern - not corporate, not academic What I Need: One front cover in high resolution Delivered as both a print-ready PDF and a screen-optimised PNG/JPG A5 format (148mm x 210mm) with 3mm bleed for print Timeline and Questions: Happy to discuss timeline and pricing. Please share examples of previous book cover work if available.

brief: I'm looking for a top-tier book cover designer for Stop Fixing Yourself! Why fixing yourself keeps the problem alive. Disruptive anti-self-help book challenging modern therapy clichés, the wellness industry, and toxic positivity. Croatian edition is selling out in physical bookstores. American edition launches on Amazon 2026. Strategic context (read first) This book belongs on the shelf next to Mark Manson, Ryan Holiday, Robert Greene, Cal Newport. It does not belong near Brené Brown, Atomic Habits, or Mel Robbins. The cover must communicate authority and seriousness, not relief or transformation. My reader has already read everything, tried every method, and is still stuck. The cover should make that reader feel seen, not promised to. Critical differentiation The Croatian edition uses a black cover with broken red chains. The American edition cannot copy this. Broken chains have become a cliché in the Amazon anxiety category and would position the book inside the genre it's critiquing. Push the chain metaphor harder and stranger, or replace it entirely with a stronger concept. If you go with chains, they cannot look like any current anxiety book on Amazon. If you go without, typography and composition must carry the full weight. Visual direction Minimalist, bold, premium, slightly dark. Deep blacks, dark greys, high-contrast typography. References in spirit: Holiday, Manson, Greene, Newport. Typography-driven covers usually outperform illustration-driven ones in this category. Strong typographic treatment on dark background with one striking element (or none). Strictly avoid No lotus flowers, sunrises, meditating people, or mountain silhouettes. No smoke, magic wands, mysterious eyes, or hypnosis-as-magic-show imagery. No motivational stock. No bright pastels. No generic broken-chain illustrations that look like every other anxiety book. Filter element on the front cover Include a small but visible line on the front, positioned subtly (above or below title, or as a small badge/stamp): No techniques. No exercises. No protocols. This is a deliberate filter that tells the wrong reader to put the book down. Small enough not to compete with the title, visible at Amazon thumbnail size. Typography Modern impactful sans-serif or elegant modern serif. Author name is Timoteo Crnković with Croatian diacritics. Font must natively support č, š, ć, ž without distortion or manual kerning. Non-negotiable. The Croatian rendering is also a deliberate signal of European authorship in a market saturated with generic American self-help, so it stays. References Three covers I want this to live next to: Mark Manson Everything Is Fucked, Ryan Holiday The Obstacle Is the Way, Cal Newport Deep Work. Croatian edition for reference only, do not replicate: https://prestaniraditinasebi.com https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Keovs434aTAhTvvM57Cbpq9pwnadL29N Audience Adults 28 to 55 in US, UK, Canada, Australia. Have read multiple self-help books. Familiar with CBT, mindfulness, breathwork, somatic experiencing. Frustrated with the genre but still buying. Not looking for another method. Looking for a different conversation. Deliverables Kindle eBook cover, high-res JPG to current KDP specs. Amazon KDP paperback full cover (front, spine, back) to exact KDP bleed specs. Page count provided once layout is final. Include space for KDP barcode/ISBN, publisher logo (Mental Reset d.o.o.), and one to two QR codes. Back cover must accommodate ~150 to 200 words of body copy, one pull quote, brief author bio, and optional author photo. Copy provided separately. Fully layered source files (PSD or AI), fonts embedded or clearly listed. Three to five high-quality 3D book mockups for marketing, social media, and stopfixingyourself.com. How to apply Start your proposal with the word RESET. Portfolio must specifically highlight dark, minimalist, or premium typography-driven covers. Prioritize examples in non-fiction, philosophy, business, or psychology. Generic portfolios will not be considered.

input_assets: Artist 'Bastian', single 'Vision Board' (aspirational 'white-collar rap') | Mood: scenic realism, cinematic, Mediterranean/aspirational-travel, warm light | 3000x3000 px min, 1:1, RGB; high-quality JPG/PNG + layered source | No URLs/handles/logos/pricing text in the artwork; all imagery/fonts licensed | Imagery from Adobe Stock (no bespoke illustration)
brief: Cover Art Designer — Hip-Hop Single (Paid Trial → Ongoing Work) Job Title (for the Upwork listing) Cover Artist for Hip-Hop Single — "Vision Board" (Trial Project, Long-Term Potential) About the project I'm 28, an independent hip-hop artist (stage name Bastian) with a catalog of 30+ tracks now rolling out as singles through a global distributor. I'm looking for a designer to create the cover art for my upcoming single "Vision Board," and — if it's a strong fit — to become my go-to cover artist for every release going forward. To be upfront: this is a paid trial. I'm hiring a few designers for this first cover so I can find the one person I want to build a long-term relationship with. If we click, you become my dedicated cover artist — regular, recurring, paid work on a predictable release schedule. I'd much rather find one great collaborator than re-hire from scratch every single. A bit about me (so you know what you're working with) I'm not your typical hip-hop artist. I grew up homeless in New York City, mostly Harlem, so grit is in my bones haha. Early on I was working jobs just to get by: working as an extra on TV shows/movies, doing sales at a gym in Spanish Harlem, construction, etc. I’m a hiphop head by nature, grew up listening to Biggie, Tupac, 50, early days Drake, MobbDeep. I dropped out of college and dove into entrepreneurship, and since 2017 I’ve been building businesses in the online/digital marketing space, while simultaneously perfecting my songwriting and lyrical skills as a hip hop artist. I know the hustle and grind intimately of being an entrepreneur, building your own business, and trying to make it. I’ve been blessed to have some pretty big wins in my journey so far, along with plenty of f***-ups, and my music is my space for me to share both. The song: "Vision Board" "Vision Board" is aspirational, motivational hip-hop - what I'd call "white collar rap." It's about manifesting a bigger life from humble beginnings: came from nothing, bootstrapped businesses, and is now experiencing the world fully. The energy is confident and earned, not flashy for its own sake. Lyrical world / imagery to pull from: "7 figures all on my vision board" "Experience life to the fullest, that's what I'm living for" Bucket-list cities, flights, "cloud skipping" Mediterranean settings — Tuscany, swimming in the Med, high-rise hotels, fine dining Came from poverty, built it in private, "putting the work in indoors" Quiet luxury and discipline — espresso with cinnamon, not champagne showers My overall visual aesthetic across the catalog is what I call "scenic realism" — cinematic, grounded, real-world atmosphere over cartoonish or overly digital looks. Here's how the song factors in: I'm keeping the track private until release, so it isn't linked here. But if this brief lights you up and you apply, I'll send you a private link to the full song (and the complete lyrics) so you can actually sit with it. Seeing the concept you come back with - after you've heard it - is a big part of how I'll decide who I book for the paid trial. Creative direction I want to see your interpretation, so I'm giving you the world of the song rather than dictating the exact image. A few guardrails: Lean into: cinematic/photographic feel, warmth, Mediterranean or aspirational-travel atmosphere, a sense of "earned" success, mood and depth, strong use of light. Avoid: generic "money/cars/chains" rap clichés, cheesy stock-photo collages, busy/cluttered compositions, anything that looks like a default Canva template. Surprise me - but make it feel like the song. Deliverables Final cover art as a high-resolution square image (see specs below) The layered source file (PSD / AI / or editable equivalent) 1–2 alternate concepts or variations if you have them (optional but a plus) Technical specs (streaming/DSP-ready) Dimensions: 3000 × 3000 px minimum, 1:1 square Color mode: RGB Format: high-quality JPG or PNG (flattened final, plus layered source) No website URLs, social handles, streaming-service logos, or promotional/pricing text in the artwork All imagery, fonts, and assets must be fully licensed for commercial use — please confirm (I'll confirm any additional distributor requirements before final delivery.) Timeline & budget Trial budget: $125 for this first cover Deadline for first draft: 24-48hrs after starting the project Ongoing rate for the long-term relationship is open to discussion once we've worked together. What I'm looking for in the right person A genuine eye — a portfolio that shows range and taste, not just technical skill Reliability and clear communication (this is recurring work; I need someone I can count on) Ability to take a creative concept and run with it Bonus: experience with music cover art, album artwork, or editorial/cinematic imagery How to apply This runs in two steps: Step 1 — Raise your hand. In your application, tell me: Why this resonates with you — what about the project, the story, or the world of the song makes you want in A link to your portfolio (and any music / album cover art you've done specifically) Your rate and turnaround for a single cover Step 2 — Hear it, then pitch a concept. If your application stands out, I'll send you a private link to the full track and lyrics. You sit with the song and come back with your concept and direction for the cover. The designers whose concepts I'm most excited about get booked for the paid trial. Please start your application with the word "7FIGS" so I know you read the full brief. Applications that don't will be skipped. I'll be reviewing portfolios closely and moving quickly. Looking forward to seeing your work.

brief: I have a series of raw lesson notes, worksheets, and short activities that need to be turned into a fully designed slide deck in both English and Arabic. The tone should feel colorful and vibrant—bright palettes, playful shapes, and big, readable type—yet still organised so teachers can move through each lesson smoothly.    The audience is children roughly 5 – 15 years old, so every slide has to balance clear learning objectives with an inviting look that keeps younger students focused. Because the deck is bilingual, I need someone comfortable aligning right-to-left Arabic alongside left-to-right English, making sure typography, spacing, and icons never feel cramped or uneven.  Here’s what I’ll provide: • Plain text content, any illustrations or logos I already have, and a rough slide outline.   • Immediate feedback as you work, since the timeline is tight.    Here’s what I need back: • An editable presentation file (PowerPoint or Google Slides) laid out in both languages.   • Consistent master layouts, title and content styles, and a simple colour guide so future lessons match perfectly.   • All fonts, images, and icons packaged or linked with full usage rights.    Turnaround is important, but I’m flexible on hours—as long as you communicate progress and hit agreed milestones. If you’ve created bilingual or educational slides before, especially for this age group, attach a few of your best samples with your proposal and note any tools you prefer to work in (PowerPoint, Google Slides, Figma, etc.). We’ll discuss timelines, milestones, and a fair rate once I’ve seen your portfolio.

brief: Scope of Work Premium Executive Presentation Design We are seeking a highly creative and experienced presentation designer to create a world-class keynote presentation related to digital transformation, governance, operational ecosystems, and smart regulatory enablement. This is NOT a standard corporate PowerPoint presentation. The objective is to create a visually stunning, cinematic, modern, and globally competitive presentation that tells a strategic transformation story similar to presentations produced for major technology conferences and global innovation forums. Presentation Objective The presentation should communicate how a large-scale national initiative transformed complex regulatory and operational activities into a fully connected digital ecosystem that supports: Consumer trust Fair trade Operational transparency Digital governance Nationwide scalability Real-time supervision and traceability The presentation should feel inspirational, futuristic, and executive-level. Required Presentation Style The desired style should resemble: Apple keynote presentations Deloitte / Accenture innovation presentations Global technology conference presentations Smart city / digital transformation storytelling The presentation should focus heavily on: Visual storytelling Minimal text Strong typography Premium animations/transitions Data visualization Futuristic ecosystem visuals High-end infographic design Cinematic flow Presentation Structure The presentation will include approximately 12–14 slides covering topics such as: The importance of trust and measurement systems National-scale transformation challenges Digital ecosystem enablement Lifecycle governance Unified operational platforms Real-time supervision and traceability National impact and measurable results Innovation and technology Future vision and smart operations A detailed content outline and messaging direction will be provided. The designer is expected to creatively transform the content into an engaging visual experience — not simply place text onto slides. Designer Responsibilities The selected designer will be responsible for: Full presentation visual design Creative direction execution Slide layout creation Infographic development Visual storytelling implementation Data visualization Animation and transition design Icon selection/design Image sourcing and visual treatment Consistent visual identity across all slides The designer is expected to propose creative visual concepts and elevate the presentation experience. Design Requirements The presentation should include: Premium modern layouts Dark futuristic theme Smart digital ecosystem visuals Minimalist high-end design Strong visual hierarchy Dynamic infographic elements Smooth and professional transitions High-quality custom visual compositions The presentation must NOT look like: Generic templates Traditional corporate/government presentations Basic infographic slides Text-heavy PowerPoint slides Deliverables The final deliverables must include: Fully editable PowerPoint presentation (.PPTX) All source files Linked assets/fonts (if applicable) High-resolution visuals Custom infographic elements Animation/transitions included Presentation optimized for large conference screen display Revisions The project must include: Minimum of 4 revision rounds Full implementation of feedback/comments Collaboration until final approval Preferred Qualifications Preference will be given to designers with experience in: Executive presentations Technology conference presentations Digital transformation presentations Smart city / AI / innovation presentations Premium infographic storytelling Please include: Portfolio samples Similar presentation work Estimated timeline Proposed creative approach Important Notes The quality standard is extremely high, and exceptional creativity and execution are required. The final presentation should feel modern, cinematic, globally competitive, and visually unforgettable. More detailed content and presentation direction will be shared with the selected designer.

brief: Job description We are looking for a professional PowerPoint designer who can recreate an existing image based presentation into a fully editable, high quality PowerPoint file with clean layout, professional animation, and modern visual design. The current presentation is available as images or screenshots. The selected freelancer must convert each slide into an editable PowerPoint format, not just place the image inside PowerPoint. Scope of work The freelancer will recreate the presentation using editable PowerPoint elements, including: Editable text boxes Editable shapes and cards Editable icons and graphic elements Editable tables, charts, timelines, workflow diagrams, and infographics Professional slide layout and alignment Consistent colors, fonts, spacing, and branding Smooth slide transitions Simple professional animations for text, cards, icons, charts, and process flows Master slide setup for consistent branding Final PowerPoint file ready for presentation and future editing Design style required The design should follow a modern corporate presentation style with: Clean and professional layout High fidelity UI and UX inspired presentation style Figma style visual structure Pixel perfect alignment Modern dashboard style elements where needed Professional use of icons, cards, tabs, charts, and section dividers Consistent brand color system Clear visual hierarchy Suitable design for investors, donors, government institutions, and business partners Animation requirements The PowerPoint should include professional but not excessive animation, such as: Fade in text animation Smooth entrance animation for cards and icons Step by step process animation Animated arrows or workflow movement where suitable Section transition effects Dashboard or data reveal animation Clean closing slide animation Animations must be simple, elegant, and suitable for formal business presentations. Deliverables The freelancer must provide: Fully editable PowerPoint file in PPTX format All slides recreated from the image based presentation Editable slide master Editable charts, shapes, icons, and text Animated version of the presentation Clean final version without design errors PDF export version Source assets used, where applicable Important requirements Please do not simply paste the image screenshots into PowerPoint. Each slide must be rebuilt as an editable presentation using PowerPoint objects, text layers, shapes, icons, tables, charts, and design components. The final presentation must allow us to easily edit text, colors, icons, layout, and animation later. Freelancer skills required The ideal freelancer should have strong experience in: Microsoft PowerPoint advanced design Corporate presentation design PowerPoint animation Pitch deck design Image to editable PowerPoint conversion Figma style UI presentation design Business proposal and donor presentation formatting Infographic design Slide master design Professional layout and visual storytelling Application instructions Please include the following in your proposal: Examples of previous editable PowerPoint presentations Examples of animated PowerPoint work Experience converting image based slides into editable PPTX Your estimated delivery time Your price for recreating the presentation Confirmation that all slides will be fully editable Preferred freelancer We prefer a freelancer who can understand corporate, government, donor, and technology sector presentations. Experience with professional company profiles, software platform presentations, investment decks, or institutional proposals will be an advantage. brief: **Brand Designer Needed: Build Complete Brochure System for Architectural Products Company** We're Sundance Architectural Products (sdap.com), an Orlando-based specialty contractor that designs, engineers, fabricates, and installs exterior architectural elements, ACM cladding, canopies, pergolas, themed structures, sunshades, and more. Our clients are architects, general contractors, commercial developers, and theme park developers. We need a designer to build out our complete brochure system from the ground up. We have finalized brand assets (logo, colors, fonts) and ~50–100 high-res project photos ready to provide. --- **SCOPE OF WORK** **1. Brand Foundation** - Create master brand template system using our finalized brand assets - Organize and tag our project photography library - Build reusable design components / layout grids that ensure consistency across the suite **2. Brochure Design Suite** - **Product-specific brochures (2–4 pages each):** Estimated 10–12 brochures covering ACM cladding, cabanas, pergolas, sunshades, themed structures, metal canopies, polycarbonate canopies, Signs, and decorative panels. - **Audience-specific brochures (4–6 pages each):** Four tailored brochures — Architects, General Contractors, Commercial Property Owners. - **Full Capability Brochure (12–16 pages):** Comprehensive company overview showing our full product/service range. **3. Deliverables** - Print-ready PDFs with bleed marks and CMYK color profile - Digital PDFs optimized for email and web - Editable master source files (in whatever tool you propose — see below) - A short Loom walkthrough showing us how to make basic edits ourselves (text swaps, photo swaps, contact info updates) --- **IMPORTANT: TOOL FLEXIBILITY** We're open to your recommendation on design software (InDesign, Canva, Affinity Publisher, etc.). However, **a major requirement is that we be able to make minor in-house edits without a designer.** In your proposal, please tell us: - What tool you'd use and why - What's required on our end to make basic edits afterward (software subscriptions, training, etc.) - Trade-offs of your recommended approach --- **REQUIRED SKILLS** - Strong portfolio of B2B brochures, ideally in architecture, construction, building products, or industrial design - Print production expertise (bleeds, CMYK, resolution, prepress) - Clean, architectural design sensibility, minimalist, photo-forward, no stock-template feel - Excellent typography and grid systems - Multi-page document design experience **NICE TO HAVE** - Background designing for architecture/AEC industry - Experience writing or editing marketing copy (we can provide raw content but appreciate refinement) --- **TO APPLY, PLEASE INCLUDE:** 1. Links to 2–3 brochure projects from your portfolio (B2B or architecture-related preferred) 2. Your recommended design tool and a brief rationale 3. Your plan for how we'll handle in-house edits after handoff 4. Your proposed approach: What order would you tackle this in? 5. Your estimated timeline 6. Your fixed-price quote (or hourly with cap) 7. One sentence: What's the first question you'd ask us before starting? Thank you and looking forward to seeing your work. Christopher Sundance Architectural Products

brief: Title: Part-Time Canva Designer Needed for Health & Wellness Brand: Flyers, Social Media Carousels, Trifold Brochure Job Description: I am looking for a part-time Canva designer / creative assistant to help with branded design projects for a physician-led health and wellness education brand. Projects may include: • One-page flyers • Instagram/social media carousels • Pinterest graphics • Trifold brochures • Simple handouts or lead magnets • Canva templates that I can reuse and edit later This is a small ongoing role, likely less than 5 hours per week, starting with a paid trial project. If the trial goes well, I may continue with weekly design support. What I’m looking for: • Strong Canva design skills • Experience creating social media carousels and print materials • Ability to follow an existing brand kit and visual direction • Clean, modern, high-quality design style • Good sense of spacing, hierarchy, fonts, and readability • Fluent English communication • Ability to take provided copy and turn it into polished visual content • Comfortable working with health, wellness, lifestyle medicine, brain health, and educational content • Reliable turnaround and clear communication • Ability to create different design versions from existing content while staying aligned with the original message Important details: I will provide the written content, brand colors, logo, and design examples. I am not looking for medical writing or independent health claims development. This is a physician-led brand, so accuracy, clarity, and trustworthiness are very important. The designer may occasionally be asked to help turn existing content into different formats, such as converting a blog section into a carousel, flyer, handout, or brochure. However, the designer should not independently add, change, or expand medical claims. Any edited, shortened, or reformatted copy must be reviewed and approved by me before use. I need help with layout, visual design, Canva execution, brand consistency, and making materials look polished and professional. I will remain responsible for medical accuracy, final wording, and approval before anything is published or printed. All final designs must be delivered as editable Canva files/templates, not just PDFs or images. Additional requirements: • Must be comfortable working with physician-led educational content • Must be able to follow provided copy closely • Must not add unsupported health claims, statistics, guarantees, or disease-treatment language • Must use only licensed, Canva-approved, or approved brand images/graphics • Must deliver all designs as editable Canva links/files • Must keep all brand materials confidential and not reuse designs for other clients • If AI tools are used for design support, please disclose this. No AI-generated medical claims or unverified health copy should be added. Trial project may include: • 1 branded Instagram carousel, or • 1 one-page flyer, or • 1 trifold brochure draft Please include: 1. Examples of Canva designs you have created, especially carousels, flyers, brochures, or wellness/education graphics 2. Your hourly rate 3. brief: We are looking for an experienced brand identity designer to refine and professionalize an existing logo concept for a European medical organization. This is not a logo creation project. The core emblem concept is already approved and must be preserved. We are seeking refinement, professionalization, scalability improvements, typography integration, and creation of a complete logo system. Applicants who prefer creating entirely new concepts are probably not a good fit. Project Scope - Refine an existing emblem into a professional vector logo - Improve geometry, balance, scalability, and visual consistency - Create a complete logo system - Develop typography pairing and logo lockups - Ensure excellent appearance in both digital and print formats Required Deliverables - Primary logo - Horizontal logo version - Emblem-only version - Square/avatar version (LinkedIn, social media, email profiles) - Circular/seal version - Favicon version - SVG, AI, EPS, PDF, and PNG exports - Design Direction The final result should feel: - Professional - Institutional - Trustworthy - Timeless - Appropriate for a medical board, professional society, or European organization We are not looking for: - Startup aesthetics - Trendy logos - Gaming or technology branding - Creative reinterpretation without justification - Requirements Please provide: - Three examples of professional institutional logos or brand identities you have designed - An example of a project where you refined or improved an existing logo - A brief explanation of your design process A detailed design brief and reference materials will be shared with shortlisted candidates. Important: Please begin your proposal with the word: Scalpel This helps us identify applicants who have read the full description.

brief: Project Brief: Marketing Collateral & Brand Assets Deliverables: Branded Presentation Folder, One-Page Service Overview, and Branded Sample Case Notes Template. 1. Project Overview & Branding Guidelines The organization provides premier, high-quality case management services, with a significant focus on complex motor vehicle accident (MVA) cases. Our work is professional, meticulous, and deeply supportive. All design deliverables must reflect this premium standard. For branding reference, color palettes, typography, and visual tone, please utilize our website. The aesthetic should be clean, modern, trustworthy, and polished. 2. Detailed Deliverable Specifications Deliverable 1: Branded Two-Pocket Presentation Folder • Purpose: To serve as the primary physical package handed to attorneys, medical providers, and clients containing our marketing materials, assessments, and reports. • Physical Specifications: Standard 9" x 12" presentation folder with two interior pockets. The right-hand interior pocket must feature standard die-cut slits to hold a business card. • Design Requirements: • Front Cover: Minimalist, clean, and high-impact. Must prominently feature the logo, brand colors, and core tagline/aesthetic. • Back Cover: Clean design containing essential company contact information (Website, Phone, Email, Office Address) and a clean layout. • Interior Pockets: Can utilize a complementary brand color or subtle geometric/abstract pattern that aligns with the website's visual assets. • Execution Note: Please deliver print-ready files (PDF/X-1a) Deliverable 2: One-Page Business Service Overview • Purpose: A high-end, scannable "leave-behind" sheet focused specifically on our Motor Vehicle Case Management Services. • Format: Single-sided or double-sided 8.5" x 11" flyer/sell sheet (Layout choice left to designer's discretion for optimal whitespace and impact). • Key Content Blocks to Include (Placeholder text is acceptable for draft): • Header: Company branding and a compelling title (e.g., Navigating Recovery: Expert Motor Vehicle Case Management). • The Company Advantage: A brief section highlighting our professional, meticulous approach to managing complex auto injury cases. • Core Services Bullet Points: * Coordination of medical care, rehabilitation, and specialized therapies. • Attendant care assessments and documentation. • Seamless communication between medical providers, legal teams, and insurance adjusters. • Comprehensive discharge planning and home modifications tracking. • Call to Action (CTA): Prominent placement at the bottom directing readers to contact us for referrals or consultations. • Design Style: Utilize clean grids, professional iconography for service bullets, and excellent typographical hierarchy to ensure it can be scanned in under 30 seconds. Deliverable 3: Branded Sample Case Notes Document (Refinement) • Purpose: A polished, professional document used to showcase a "mock case" to attorneys and adjusters, demonstrating the exceptional detail, clarity, and quality of clients clinical documentation. • Format: Multi-page 8.5" x 11" document template (Word or editable PDF format requested for final delivery). • Project Scope: We will provide an existing, real-world sample case notes document to use as a structural baseline. Your role is not to write content, but to completely overhaul the visual design, typography, and layout to transform it into a marketing-professional, highly branded corporate asset. • Design Requirements: • Header/Footer: Professional, unified running header featuring a clean variant of the logo. Footer should include page numbering ‬and a standard confidentiality notice. • Client Information Block: Convert the existing top-page data into a beautifully structured, clean table or grid to display fields such as Client Name, Date of Injury, Claim Number, Case Manager Name, and Report Date. • Body Layout: Upgrade the typography with distinct, professional styling for headings (e.g., Clinical Assessment, Care Coordination Activities, Progress Summary, Recommended Action Plan) to ensure maximum readability. • Visual Elements: Utilize subtle brand colors for section dividers, callout boxes, or table headers to turn standard clinical notes into an executive-level report.

brief: We need a professional graphic designer / print designer to recreate a two-sided DIN A5 package insert for Blausweta-Rasur. The current visual references are included in the asset package. Please do not simply upscale the AI drafts. The task is to rebuild the front and back side cleanly in Illustrator/InDesign as a proper print-ready layout with editable text, real vector logo, clean icons, correct bleed, and final PDF export for SAXOPRINT. Blausweta-Rasur is a German specialist retailer / online shop for shaving, grooming, razors, razor blades, care products and barber supplies. The shop serves private customers, barbers, salons and resellers. Blausweta-Rasur is already established on eBay and now wants to motivate marketplace customers to order directly in the official online shop. The insert should communicate: professional specialist retailer, reliable shipping, fair prices, direct shop advantages, loyalty/bonus points, referral benefits and shop-exclusive discount codes. Main goal: Customers who may know Blausweta from eBay or another marketplace should switch to the official online shop. Important campaign message: - Customers can save more directly in the Blausweta online shop. - First direct shop order after switching from eBay or another marketplace: 7% discount with code SHOP7. - Follow-up order / next direct shop order: 5% discount with code DANKE5. - Bonus points are collected additionally and can be redeemed. - Referral discounts are also available. Important: All discounts are percentages. Do not use € discount wording. Required deliverables: 1. Editable source file: Adobe Illustrator .ai preferred, or InDesign .indd with linked assets. 2. Print-ready two-sided PDF for SAXOPRINT, CMYK, PDF/X-4 preferred. 3. Front side and back side as separate high-resolution PNG/JPG previews. 4. All text must remain editable in the source file. 5. Use the official logo from the provided logo files. Do not trace/recreate the logo from the AI draft. 6. Use the clean warehouse header image without any baked-in logo. The logo will be positioned manually in the layout. Format / print setup: - DIN A5 flyer / package insert, double-sided, portrait orientation. - Final trim size: 148 × 210 mm. - Artwork size with bleed: 152 × 214 mm. - Bleed: 2 mm on every side. - Keep all important text/logos/icons at least 3 mm inside the final trim edge. Prefer 5 mm for safety. - Background images that touch the edge must extend into the bleed. - CMYK color mode. - Minimum image resolution: 250 dpi at final size, ideally 300 dpi. - Embed all fonts or convert to outlines in the print PDF. Design direction: Keep the style very close to the references: - Clean, modern, professional specialist retailer look. - White background with deep navy / purple accents. - No colorful mix. Stay in the Blausweta navy/purple world. - No gold, no green, no Trustpilot badge. - Avoid wedding/thank-you-card feeling. - Avoid playful hearts and handwritten romantic scripts. - The back side should feel like a professional shop benefit page. - The front side can remain warmer and personal, but still businesslike. Coupon details: SHOP7 = 7% welcome/switch discount for first direct shop order after switching from eBay or another platform. One-time use only. DANKE5 = 5% discount for the next direct shop order / follow-up order.

Preferred tools include Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve—use whichever you’re most efficient in. brief: I need a talented graphic designer to create a polished, professional invitation for a SEAL PAC fundraising event I am hosting in Mount Pleasant, South Carolina. This is a quick-turn project with a final product needed within 24 hours. I will provide the prior event invitation as a visual reference, but this needs to be updated for the new event details, sponsorship levels, venue, and RSVP/payment flow. The previous invite used a patriotic SEAL PAC theme, highlighted the host, event location, special guests, sponsorship levels, and RSVP instructions. The final design should feel patriotic, high-end, clean, and appropriate for a congressional/political fundraising event. It should be suitable for email, text message sharing, social media, and printing. Event Details: Event: SEAL PAC Fundraiser Host: Gavin McCulley Date: July 7, 2026 Time: 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM Location: Tavern & Table Shem Creek, Mount Pleasant, South Carolina Event Description: Join us for a private lunch in support of SEAL PAC, dedicated to electing conservative veterans to Congress. Guests will have the opportunity to meet and spend time with the Congressman, discuss the year ahead, and support SEAL PAC’s mission. Sponsorship Levels: $1,000 Minimum Sponsorship Includes: Lunch Photograph with the Congressman Opportunity to speak with the Congressman about the year ahead SEAL PAC souvenir, such as a hat, shirt, or other commemorative item $5,000 Sponsorship Limited to 5 people Includes everything from the $1,000 level, plus: Private dinner with the Congressman that evening at Hall’s Chophouse Dinner included RSVP / Registration: Please include space for an Eventbrite registration link or QR code. We will provide the link once created. Deliverables Needed: One polished invitation design in PDF format One high-resolution PNG or JPG for texting and social media Editable source file, preferably Canva, Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, or InDesign Version with QR code or placeholder for Eventbrite link Final files delivered within 24 hours Design Direction: Use the attached previous SEAL PAC invite as a reference for tone and general style, but improve the layout and make it feel more refined and modern. Patriotic colors are appropriate, but the final design should not feel cluttered. It should be clean, premium, and easy to read. Ideal Candidate: Experience designing political, nonprofit, military, veteran, or fundraising event materials Able to work quickly and communicate clearly Strong layout and typography skills Can deliver a professional final product within 24 hours

ASK ME.” I’m also open to incorporating phrases like “Jesus Changes Everything” or “I Believe Jesus Changes Lives.” The Pray for Kalamazoo logo must be included somewhere on the shirt, but I want it to feel subtle and well integrated into the design rather than overpowering it. I attempted designing this myself in Canva but couldn’t quite get the layout, sizing, and placement to feel cohesive. I need help creating a design that will print beautifully on a washed black t-shirt and feel like a true modern streetwear piece.

task_type: Wordmark Logo — "Modern Perfumery" (warm earthy palette, monochrome-ready)
title: Logo designer
note: Brief explicitly wants a clean wordmark and to AVOID complicated icons, so a wordmark template fits; but fill_text changes copy only — the exact refined typeface and warm-tone tuning need a human in Express.
mcp_workflow: search_design (Express refined wordmark/serif logo template) -> fill_text ("Modern Perfumery") -> image_apply_color_overlay (warm cognac/amber tone) -> change_background_color (cream + dark-cognac reversed versions) -> image_vectorize -> document_render_vector (AI/SVG + PNG)
input_assets: Brand name: "Modern Perfumery" | Palette: cognac amber, warm sand, soft cream, warm copper (no gold/black/stark white) — exact hex to confirm | Must work on cream/sand (primary), reversed on deep amber/cognac, full-colour and single warm tone (monochrome-ready), favicon to banner scale | Refined, breathable serif/editorial feel — confirm exact font
brief: Who We Are Modern Perfumery is a Scandinavian-based fragrance brand built for people who take scent seriously. We are the go-to destination for premium and niche perfumes in the Nordic market — curating the best fragrances in the world and making them accessible to a new generation of perfume lovers in Denmark and across Scandinavia. We are not a department store. We are not fast fashion for fragrance. We are a world built entirely around the art, knowledge, and culture of perfume. What We Do We curate, sell, and educate. Our platform — built primarily through Instagram and online retail — is a universe dedicated to perfume. We share the stories behind fragrances, the craft behind the ingredients, the culture behind the houses. And we sell the pieces worth owning. Think of us as the place where someone goes when they want to understand perfume — not just wear it. Our Market We operate online, serving customers across Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. Scandinavia is experiencing a growing wave of interest in niche and premium fragrances — and there is a significant gap in the market for a brand that speaks to this audience with authority, taste, and warmth. We are here to fill that gap. Brand Personality Warm Not cold luxury. Not intimidating. Approachable but elevated. We want people to feel welcomed into this world, not excluded from it. Knowledgeable We know this world deeply. Our audience trusts us because we speak with genuine authority — not because we shout the loudest. Curated We do not sell everything. We sell the right things. Every fragrance we feature has earned its place. Modern We bring the world of niche perfumery into a contemporary, digital-first space. Clean, considered, and current — never stuffy or old-fashioned. Grounded There is an earthiness to what we do — amber, oud, resin, wood. Our aesthetic is rooted in nature, warmth, and depth. Nothing artificial. Nothing cold. Visual Direction The aesthetic reference is warm afternoon desert light — think amber glass, cream linen, warm sand, and soft shadows. Think quiet luxury. The kind that does not need to announce itself. The mood is: a beautifully lit room that smells extraordinary. Unhurried. Rich. Considered. References in this direction: high-end fragrance houses with a warm, editorial aesthetic. Nothing clinical, nothing cold, nothing that belongs in a pharmacy. Color Palette We want warm, natural, and earthy tones — think cognac amber, warm sand, soft cream, and warm copper. The palette should feel like afternoon light through amber glass. We do not want anything with gold, black, or hard coloring. These feel too generic, too flashy, or too heavy for what we are building. The brand should feel warm and breathable — never cold, never loud. Logo Direction The name is Modern Perfumery. We want the logo to feel timeless, not trendy. It must work across all contexts: on a cream background, on a dark background, on packaging, on a website header, on a phone screen, and potentially on a physical label. What We Do Not Want — Anything that looks generic or mass-market — Decorative flourishes that feel dated or feminine in a clichéd way — Anything that could belong to a drugstore, a spa brand, or a candle company — Overly complicated symbols or icons — Anything shiny, metallic, or flashy — Stark black or bright white — always warm tones Typography Feeling The typography should feel refined but breathable. Not heavy. Not condensed. Imagine a high-end fragrance house meets a Scandinavian editorial brand. Clean lines, warm tone, quiet confidence. How the Logo Should Work — On cream or sand backgrounds — primary use — Reversed out on deep amber or dark cognac — for packaging — In full color and in a single warm tone (monochrome-ready) — At small sizes on a phone screen and at large sizes on a website banner In One Sentence Modern Perfumery is where serious fragrance culture meets the modern Scandinavian consumer — warm, knowledgeable, curated, and built to become the defining perfume brand in the North.

brief: Signage & Wayfinding Graphic Designer (Project-Based / Ongoing Collaboration) I am looking to collaborate with a freelance designer to support the development of signage and wayfinding systems for interior-led projects. The work focuses on creating clear, refined, and cohesive signage concepts that align with the spatial and architectural language of each project (residential and commercial). This is a project-based role with the potential for ongoing collaboration across multiple developments. Scope of Work *Developing a concept based on the look and feel of the building Architecture or idea shared. * Developing signage layouts based on the established concept direction * Designing room signage, numbering systems, and directional elements * Applying typography, spacing, and hierarchy consistently across all signage types * Creating simple iconography where required (aligned with the concept language) * Preparing clean, production-ready artwork files * Supporting minor revisions based on feedback Project Context Typical signage scope may include: * Floor identification and numbering * Apartment / unit numbers * Amenity signage (gym, pool, shared spaces) * Service areas (utility rooms, back-of-house) * Directional signage All projects are interior-focused, requiring a clean, modern, and well-resolved visual approach. What I’am Looking For * Strong layout and typography skills * Experience in signage, wayfinding, or environmental graphics (preferred) * Ability to work within an existing design system and maintain consistency * Attention to detail and alignment * Clear communication and reliability with deadlines Working Structure * Design direction will be provided before starting work. * Work will be assigned per project or per signage package Please share: * Portfolio (relevant signage / graphic / layout work preferred) * Brief introduction * Your typical freelance rates or preferred pricing structure

brief: I need a professionally designed menu that will live entirely online, so everything you create must be optimised for digital use—think sharp on retina displays, quick to load, and easy to read on phones, tablets, and desktop screens alike. I will supply the dish names, descriptions, prices, and any brand assets (logo, colour palette, preferred fonts). What I want from you is a cohesive layout that makes the food shine, guides the reader’s eye naturally, and fits neatly within the overall brand look.  Because the project is digital-only, please keep print-specific considerations out unless you feel a dual-purpose file would add value; if you can include that as a bonus, let me know. Your design tools are up to you—Adobe InDesign, Illustrator, Figma, Canva Pro, or any other professional platform you prefer—so long as the final files meet the requirements below.  Deliverables: • Editable source file (AI, PSD, INDD, or Figma).   • Ready-to-publish PDF plus high-resolution PNG/JPEG slices for web and social media.   • Web-friendly typography or embedded fonts to avoid display issues.    I’d like two rounds of tweaks after the first draft so we can nail every detail, then a quick hand-off with clear instructions for any future updates. If this sounds like your next design win, tell me how you’ll approach the layout and any previous digital menus you’ve crafted.

brief: PROJECT: THE GEORGE INN OUNDLE – MENU DESIGN  I need a professional tri-fold (3-panel foldable) menu design created in Canva for The George Inn, Oundle.  The design should be fully editable in Canva so that we can easily update prices, add or remove products, and print new copies in the future without requiring further design work.  DELIVERABLES  1. Main Drinks Menu (Tri-Fold) Draught Beer & Cider Bottled Beer & Cider Gin Vodka Rum Whisky & Bourbon Brandy & Cognac Tequila, Shots & Liqueurs Soft Drinks Mixers Juices  2. Wine List (Tri-Fold) White Wines Rosé Wines Red Wines Premium Wines Sparkling Wines Prosecco Port Wine by the Glass & Bottle Pricing  3. Cocktail Menu (Tri-Fold)  Signature Cocktails Classic Cocktails Spritz Selection Mocktails Premium Serves Seasonal Specials  DESIGN REQUIREMENTS  Use The George Inn Oundle logo provided. Traditional premium British pub/gastro pub style. Elegant and timeless design. Suitable for table presentation and printing. Consistent branding across all three menus. Professional typography with clear pricing. High readability. Print-ready layout. Include page margins and bleed considerations. Canva editable source files must be supplied. All fonts and elements used must be available within Canva. Design should be easy to update in future.  COLOUR PALETTE Traditional pub styling. Cream/parchment background. Dark green accents. Black text. Optional subtle gold highlights.  FILES REQUIRED Canva editable link/file. Print-ready PDF versions. High-resolution PNG exports.  The final design should feel similar to a premium countryside pub, gastro pub, or boutique inn rather than a modern nightclub or chain restaurant.

brief: I’m building a series of Instagram Reels shot across Nepal and I want each clip to feel polished, immersive, and cinematic. The footage ranges from spontaneous street moments to sweeping mountain drone shots—so it doesn’t neatly fit into the usual “travel, culture, or food” buckets. Here’s the editing approach I need: • Overall look: smooth, cinematic pacing rather than frenetic jump-cuts.   • Must-have touches: tasteful slow-motion effects where they elevate emotion, plus seamless transitions that keep scenes flowing organically.   • Consistent grade: warm, true-to-life tones that celebrate Nepal’s natural colours without over-saturation.  Typical workload will be 3–5 Reels per batch, each 25–30 seconds, delivered in vertical 9:16, ready for upload (H.264, 1080 × 1920). Please supply both the final exports and the project files so I can tweak captions later if needed.  Acceptance criteria:   1. Every Reel opens with an attention-grabbing frame and retains smooth motion throughout.   2. Slow-mo segments are stabilised and properly blended with real-time footage.   3. Transitions feel invisible—no hard cuts unless they serve a specific beat.   4. Colour grading is consistent across the batch.   5. Final files pass Instagram’s compression without noticeable artefacts.  If this sounds like your style, send over a quick sample Reel or portfolio link that shows cinematic editing with slow-mo and seamless transitions. Excited to collaborate and showcase Nepal beautifully.
